item.title: Trump alone can’t save college sports. But Congress can.
item.description: The SCORE Act would help civilize the Wild West that student-athletes have been living in for years.

item.title: Before Washington could lead America, he had to learn to lose
item.description: New York was Washington’s crucible.

item.title: The Justice Department’s position on presidential papers is astounding
item.description: I know the Presidential Records Act well. Now the Trump administration is calling it unconstitutional.

item.title: Can you spot a fake political ad? AI is making it harder.
item.description: Voters will need to adopt a “buyer beware” mentality.

item.title: Lebanon gets a second chance to do the right thing
item.description: Sympathy for Lebanon that ignores the government’s complicity is counterproductive.

item.title: Presidential libraries, or monuments to our leaders’ egomania?
item.description: What started as places to store presidential papers for historians have turned into flashy personal museums.

item.title: The raw milk movement is dumb and dangerous, but don’t overreact
item.description: Drinking unpasteurized milk is risky, but there are bigger public health battles to fight.

item.title: America’s income tax is progressive
item.description: The rich already pay more than their fair share.

item.title: Targeting the Vatican
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Pope Leo XIV, Donald Trump, Iran war.

item.title: Sen. Richard Blumenthal: Don’t nickel-and-dime veterans
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal on veteran spending. Readers on NASA.

item.title: A deserved defeat on NIH funding cuts
item.description: DOGE tried to slash research grants by targeting “indirect costs.” A court ruled that to be illegal.

item.title: The lessons of Viktor Orban’s defeat
item.description: Corruption and stagnation led to the Hungarian prime minister’s landslide loss.

item.title: Trump ally Viktor Orban loses in Hungary
item.description:
An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.
00:00 - Monologue 03:13 - Trump ally Viktor Orban loses in Hungary 19:29 - Trump sends Navy to blockade the Strait of Hormuz 33:51 - Will Eric Swalwell step down after accusations?

item.title: A spirited tax ruling protects homemade whiskey
item.description: A toast to an appeals court that says the federal government can’t ban at-home distilleries.

item.title: Trump flips the script in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The ceasefire gave the president an opening. He used it to turn Iran’s strategy back on itself.

item.title: Orban’s defeat shows the Achilles’ heel of populist power
item.description: Formerly ascendant European right-wing parties are in retreat, a possible omen for Trump’s MAGA movement.

item.title: On war, peace, the president and the pope
item.description: It’s a shame the Trump vs. Pope Leo mess makes serious discussion of “just war” theory impossible.

item.title: What’s behind the staggering drop in U.S. public companies
item.description: The hostile litigation environment amounts to a de facto tax on participating in public markets.

item.title: Why honest students fear AI detectors
item.description: Chatbot detectors are unreliable and their use threatens honest students.

item.title: Maryland’s a lot like Minnesota. It needs a statewide watchdog.
item.description: A patchwork of small oversight units across agencies is no substitute for a strong centralized office.

item.title: The old U.S. energy fight is collapsing under threat from Iran
item.description: Energy solutions should come from all sources, not just those favored by one political party.

item.title: Trump’s Fed chair nominee is pushing a myth
item.description: The Fed chair nominee says Alan Greenspan trusted anecdotes over data. That’s not true.

item.title: A giant barrier to being self-employed is falling, state by state
item.description: Welcome to the “portable benefits” revolution.

item.title: What really happened in Islamabad — and what Trump is trying now
item.description: The president is betting economic pressure can do what bombs couldn’t.

item.title: Big spending on social housing is untenable in this economy
item.description: The D.C. mayoral candidate’s dueling plans: Bold and unrealistic vs. modest and achievable.

item.title: The U.S. needs another president like William Henry Harrison
item.description: Readers on the ninth and 10th presidents, and the assimilation bargain.

item.title: The U.S. military is missing out because of Hegseth’s war on Anthropic
item.description: The new Mythos model can detect critical software flaws, but the Pentagon is barring itself from using it.

item.title: Why the Iran negotiations went nowhere. For now.
item.description: Are talks over or just starting? With Iran, it’s hard to tell. Both sides’ wild rhetoric doesn’t help.
